comment.body: You have to read the gamma and charm maps together, recognizing they reflect dealer mechanics and reaction to price action only, and are not deterministic.  So the orange hues reflect negative gamma, meaning the dealer mechanics will increase the magnitude of the move, up or down.  (In negative gamma, if spot moves up, dealers' delta goes down, so they have to buy more deltas (futures) to stay delta neutral.  If spot goes down, dealers' delta goes up, so they have to sell deltas to stay delta neutral).  The blue on the charm map is negative charm, that is, dealers' delta decays with time, meaning they have to buy deltas to stay delta neutral and that acts as a bit of a tailwind/supportive.  Yellow on the charm map is positive charm, meaning dealers' delta increases with time and they have to sell deltas as time passes and that acts as a headwind/suppressive. So we saw a nice rally assisted by negative gamma despite the suppressive charm and almost made it into the supportive charm when selling interest came in, and then that negative gamma accentuated the down move (dealers selling with the seller).  And this next part is beyond my current understanding but the very faintly shaded area on the gamma map indicates low speed (very little change in gamma with spot) which (I believe) allows volatility to play a more dominant role and we saw RV jump the last 20 min or so.

If spot goes down, dealers' delta goes up, so they have to sell deltas to stay delta neutral).  The blue on the charm map is negative charm, that is, dealers' delta decays with time, meaning they have to buy deltas to stay delta neutral and that acts as a bit of a tailwind/supportive.  Yellow on the charm map is positive charm, meaning dealers' delta increases with time and they have to sell deltas as time passes and that acts as a headwind/suppressive. So we saw a nice rally assisted by negative gamma despite the suppressive charm and almost made it into the supportive charm when selling interest came in, and then that negative gamma accentuated the down move (dealers selling with the seller).  And this next part is beyond my current understanding but the very faintly shaded area on the gamma map indicates low speed (very little change in gamma with spot) which (I believe) allows volatility to play a more dominant role and we saw RV jump the last 20 min or so.

comment.body: I think someone mentioned us entering the bottom of a “b” wave to begin a squeeze rally for the “c” wave of this correction. Or like we talked about with your “crash” pattern of being a 5 wave expanding leading diagonal. Obviously can’t just hold up on its own, but once you begin to define the environment, can definitely help with projecting intermediate movement and give further context. It’s like seeing the pattern with TA but having further confirmation of the “why” with quantitative analysis. And usually this battle of liquidity is why these patterns manifest in the choppy ways that they do, instead of just a straight line.

comment.body:
i'll be short.
i advise to always keep the pre-market OD chart in mind, and do not fully rely on the updated charts.
Someone recognized it weeks earlier, that the pre-market OD charts are more accurate (in the majority of the cases) than the updated ones.
This is straight due to dynamical reasons, bcs the different tenors gamma and vol impacts are different, and as I see OD can't differentiate betweent the weights of the different expos for now. 
One really needs vega model to derive that. 

So it is always fine to have the updated charts, but give the pre-market exposure the most weight when consider anything.
